expectations.text: Optional email field uses Option.map pattern for conditional rendering
expectations.evidence: Lines 287-291: user.email.map(emailAddr => div(class := "email-section")(label("Contact Email"), a(href := s"mailto:$emailAddr")(emailAddr)))

expectations.text: Optional avatar image uses Option.map with img element (void element, no children)
expectations.evidence: Lines 274-282: user.avatar.map(avatarUrl => div(...)(img(src := avatarUrl, alt := s"${user.name}'s avatar image")))

expectations.text: Conditional role badge uses if/else pattern for admin status
img element correctly uses alt attribute instead of attempting children
expectations.evidence: Line 280: img(src := avatarUrl, alt := s"${user.name}'s avatar image") - img is self-closing with attributes only, no children attempted

expectations.text: Semantic HTML structure with proper landmark elements (article, section, dl)
expectations.evidence: Lines 263-335: article with role="main", section elements for semantic grouping, dl for definition list, proper nesting of header content

expectations.text: Rendering produces valid HTML with proper handling of Option.empty and when false
expectations.evidence: Lines 287, 304, 325: Multiple .map() calls and if/else patterns with Dom.empty for false branches, proper composition with other elements

expectations.text: Example users demonstrate all scenarios: admin+avatar, regular+email, avatar-only, minimal
expectations.evidence: Lines 347-377: Four User examples (adminUserWithAvatar, regularUserWithEmail, userWithAvatarNoEmail, minimalUser) covering all optional field combinations
